svg.text: All flows traced from actual code · Every step shows the real function and endpoint
Latency: RTSP capture (0ms) → ONNX inference (~45ms) → validation (~5ms) → upload (~100ms) → cloud store (~50ms) → WebSocket (~10ms) = ~210ms end-to-end
edge_service.store_detection() → detection_logs (MongoDB) + frame (S3) → incident_service.create_or_update() → websockets.publish() → notification_service.dispatch()
Admin click → Roboflow API → .pt download → ultralytics ONNX export → S3 upload → model_versions DB → Celery OTA → edge command → presigned download → ONNX session swap
Class sync: model.names extracted → detection_classes collection updated → edge receives update_classes command → ALERT_CLASSES refreshed dynamically
Roles: super_admin > org_admin > operator > ml_engineer > store_owner > viewer | Lockout after N failed attempts | JTI blacklist for logout
Retry: max 2 attempts with 30s delay | Dedup: skip same (incident, rule, recipient) within window | Quiet hours: per-rule configurable
Models: orgs/{org}/models/{version}.onnx | Clips: orgs/{org}/clips/{cam}/{ts}.mp4 | Sig v4 auth
Celery sync_to_roboflow() → batch upload to Roboflow workspace → COCO export reads from detection_classes DB
Annotation: manual labels → dataset_frames + annotations collections → exportable for retraining
FloorEye v3.0.0 — Data Flow Diagrams — Generated from codebase analysis — 2026-03-29
All function names, endpoints, and data transformations verified by reading actual source code
